source: American Diabetes Association — Economic Costs of Diabetes in the U.S. in 2022 (Parker et al., Diabetes Care 46:1, 2024)
notes: Mean total direct medical expenditure per diagnosed-T2D adult, USD 2022. Use as cost weight for the S1 (Uncontrolled T2D) Markov state.

source: AHRQ Medical Expenditure Panel Survey — Medical Conditions Tables (T2D, well-controlled subset, 2021)
notes: Mean MEPS-reported annual medical spend for adults with well-controlled T2D (HbA1c < 7). Use as cost weight for S0 (Controlled) state for diabetic patients on therapy.

source: USRDS 2023 Annual Data Report, Vol 1, Ch 6 — Healthcare Expenditures for Persons with CKD (Medicare FFS)
notes: Per-person-per-year Medicare FFS spend for CKD stage 3. Excludes ESRD. Used for S2 (CKD/nephropathy) state cost weight.

source: USRDS 2023 Annual Data Report, Vol 1, Ch 6
notes: Per-person-per-year Medicare FFS spend for CKD stages 4–5 (pre-dialysis). Used for S2 cost weight in Option B (5-state model).

source: USRDS 2023 Annual Data Report, Vol 2, Ch 9 — Healthcare Expenditures for Persons with ESRD
notes: PPPY Medicare FFS cost for all ESRD modalities, US 2021 data, reported in 2023 ADR. Commercial-payer ESRD cost is typically 3-4x higher (HCCI 2022); flagged as sensitivity-analysis parameter.

source: Health Care Cost Institute (HCCI) 2022 — Commercial Insurance Spending on Dialysis (Milliman analysis)
notes: PPPY commercial-insurance dialysis spend; use only when target payer is commercial (vs. Medicare).

source: Nicholson et al. — Cost of cardiovascular events in U.S. commercial claims (Am J Manag Care, 2021); CMS MEDPAR FY2022 inpatient claims for MI/stroke DRGs
notes: Average per-episode inpatient cost for non-fatal MI or stroke, US 2021–22. Applied as one-time cost when patient transitions into S4 (CV event).

source: O'Sullivan et al. — Post-MI annual healthcare cost (Circ Cardiovasc Qual Outcomes, 2011, inflated to 2022 USD)
notes: Annual maintenance cost in years following a non-fatal CV event. Adds to baseline state cost in years t>0 after CV event.

source: UKPDS Outcomes Model v2 (Hayes et al., Diabetologia 56:9, 2013); patients HbA1c ≥ 8 off therapy
notes: Annual probability that a patient with HbA1c ≥ 8 and no glucose-lowering therapy transitions from 'controlled' to 'uncontrolled' state. Conservative midpoint of UKPDS and ACCORD off-therapy arms.

source: UKPDS Outcomes Model v2 (Hayes et al., 2013)
notes: Annual progression probability for HbA1c 7.0–7.9, post-discontinuation.

source: DPP Outcomes Study 10-year follow-up (Knowler et al., Lancet 374:9702, 2009)
notes: Annual T2D incidence in pre-diabetic patients post-intervention (placebo arm). Used as S0->S1 transition for patients with HbA1c 5.7–6.4.

source: CDC National Diabetes Statistics Report 2023 — incident diabetes rate, adults with normal glycemia
notes: Annual T2D incidence in metabolically healthy adults (HbA1c < 5.7). Applied as S0->S1 transition for patients entering S0 below the pre-DM threshold.

source: Adler et al. UKPDS 64 — Development and progression of nephropathy in T2D (Kidney Int 63, 2003)
notes: Annual probability of developing microalbuminuria/CKD in T2D patients. UKPDS prospective cohort, 11.2-yr follow-up.

source: USRDS 2023 ADR, Ch 4 — CKD progression to ESRD; Adler UKPDS 64
notes: Annual progression from established diabetic CKD to ESRD requiring dialysis. Weighted average across CKD stages.

source: Framingham + ACCORD analyses; Emerging Risk Factors Collaboration (Lancet 375, 2010)
notes: Annual non-fatal CV event (MI/stroke) probability for T2D adults with poor glycemic control.

source: Emerging Risk Factors Collaboration (Lancet 375, 2010)
notes: Annual CV event probability for adults with controlled glycemia and obesity/pre-DM.

source: USRDS 2023 ADR Vol 1, Ch 4 — CV disease in CKD
notes: Annual CV event rate in CKD patients; CV risk roughly doubles vs. T2D-without-CKD.

source: CDC NCHS National Vital Statistics; mid-adult all-cause mortality, US 2022
notes: Background mortality for adults 45–65 in absorbing 'death' state. Stratify by state in v2.

source: USRDS 2023 ADR Vol 2, Ch 5 — Mortality and Causes of Death
notes: Annual mortality on dialysis (all-cause), US Medicare FFS 2021. Higher than CKD/T2D states by an order of magnitude.

source: STEP-1 1-year off-treatment extension (Wilding et al., DOM 2022, 24:1553); SUSTAIN-1 off-treatment data (Sorli et al., Lancet D&E 2017)
notes: Average monthly HbA1c rise post-GLP-1 discontinuation. Revised from 0.18 to 0.10 in Phase 2 to remain consistent with the 12-month plateau (rate*12 must be >= plateau*reduction to allow the asymptote to be reached). The shape is linear-to-plateau; biological reality is closer to monoexponential and would be a v2 upgrade.

source: STEP-1 1-year off-treatment extension (Wilding et al., Diabetes Obes Metab 2022)
notes: Fraction of GLP-1-induced HbA1c reduction that is regained by 12 months post-discontinuation. Used to cap rebound trajectory.

source: STEP-1 extension (Wilding et al., DOM 2022); SURMOUNT-4 (Aronne et al., JAMA 331, 2024)
notes: Mean monthly BMI rebound post-discontinuation of semaglutide 2.4 mg / tirzepatide. Used in rebound severity score.

source: STEP-1 extension (Wilding et al., DOM 2022)
notes: Fraction of weight loss regained 1 year after GLP-1 stopped. Caps long-horizon rebound.

source: DPP Outcomes Study 10-yr follow-up (Knowler 2009); HbA1c 6.0–6.4 stratum
notes: Annual T2D incidence for adults with HbA1c 6.0–6.4 (high-risk pre-DM).

source: DPP Outcomes Study 10-yr follow-up (Knowler 2009); HbA1c 5.7–5.9 stratum
notes: Annual T2D incidence for adults with HbA1c 5.7–5.9 (low-risk pre-DM).

source: Second Panel on Cost-Effectiveness in Health and Medicine (Sanders et al., JAMA 316:10, 2016)
notes: Recommended real annual discount rate for US health-economic analyses. Apply to all future costs in Markov rollout.

source: Convention — annual cycle Markov chain
notes: v1 uses an annual cycle. Sub-annual (3- or 6-month) cycles can be considered in v2 if rebound dynamics need finer resolution.

source: Plan §Phase 1; Open Question #1
notes: Default horizon for primary ROI reporting. 10-year horizon used as sensitivity output.

source: Novo Nordisk Ozempic WAC list price (RED BOOK 2024); list price ≈ $1,150/month × 12
notes: Wholesale Acquisition Cost — list. Net-of-rebate cost is materially lower (~30–40% off list per industry analyses). Use for upper-bound sensitivity.

source: Lilly Mounjaro/Zepbound WAC list price (RED BOOK 2024)
notes: Mounjaro/Zepbound WAC list, 2024. Same caveat re: rebates.

source: Novo Nordisk Saxenda/Victoza WAC list (RED BOOK 2024)
notes: Liraglutide is approaching generic entry; expect price decline in 2025–26.

source: Lilly Trulicity WAC list (RED BOOK 2024)
notes: WAC list 2024; net cost lower after rebates.

source: Plan §Phase 3; existing budget_impact.csv assumption (NOT externally sourced)
notes: Existing assumption in budget_impact.csv. Phase 3 should treat this as a user-configurable slider input. Flag as 'assumed, not sourced' in the parameter registry.

source: Plan §Phase 3; existing budget_impact.csv assumption (NOT externally sourced)

source: SUSTAIN-6 (Marso et al., NEJM 375, 2016) and LEADER (Marso et al., NEJM 375, 2016) cardiovascular outcomes meta

notes: Relative risk of kidney-disease progression events with semaglutide vs placebo in T2D + CKD. Apply to renal-state transitions for on-therapy patients.

notes: Mean on-therapy HbA1c reduction at steady state for semaglutide 1.0–2.4 mg. Used by rebound model as 'the loss available to rebound'.

source: SURPASS-1 to -5 program (Frias et al., NEJM 385, 2021)
notes: Mean on-therapy HbA1c reduction for tirzepatide 10–15 mg.

notes: Mean weight loss on semaglutide 2.4 mg at 68 weeks. Used by rebound model as the 'weight regain pool'.

notes: Mean weight loss on tirzepatide 15 mg at 72 weeks.
